subject: New siding? The upgrade you only get one shot at
preheader: Once the old layer comes off, what you do next decides your winter heating bills.
title: Re-siding or re-roofing? The energy decisions hiding inside
introHtml: Plenty of Cape homes will get new roofs or siding before winter. Here's the part nobody mentions: once the old layer is stripped, you're one decision away from a warmer, drier house — for the cost of materials, not a second project. That window closes the day the new layer goes on.
articleHtml:
The wall is open. Now what?

Your studs are thermal bridges — wood conducts heat far better than insulation, so every stud is a stripe of heat loss even in a well-insulated wall. The fix is continuous exterior insulation: rigid foam or mineral wool board over the whole wall before the new siding. You can only install it when the siding is off, and re-siding is that moment. Same logic up top: rigid insulation above the roof deck goes on during a re-roof or never.

Then the details that cost almost nothing while everything's exposed:

  • Housewrap as an air barrier — taped seams, sealed around windows and penetrations. Wind-washed walls lose heat; a tight wrap stops the draft before it starts.
  • A rainscreen gap behind cedar shingles or clapboard, so Cape moisture drains and the back of your siding can dry.
  • Ridge and soffit ventilation — balanced intake and exhaust keeps the roof deck cold in winter, which is what actually prevents ice dams.
  • Flashing done right — kickout flashing where roof meets wall, ice-and-water membrane at eaves and valleys, proper drip edge. Dry framing is the cheapest efficiency there is.

What does efficiency mean to you?

Lower bills? Fewer drafts in the north-facing bedroom? No more ice dams? Name your target before the tear-off — it changes the spec. A no-cost More on our approach at our services page and the blog.
tips: Ask any siding bidder how they'll detail housewrap at windows — a vague answer tells you everything.
Peek in the attic: soffit vents blocked by insulation cancel out your ridge vent.
Re-roofing? That's the day to add ice-and-water membrane at the eaves, not after the next ice dam.
ctaLine: Planning a roof or siding project for next season? Reply with your question or call (508) 566-1190 — consultations are free and estimates come back within 24 hours.
